 {"id":"bd-4g90","title":"Add btrfs hard-link directory EPERM test + ext4 cross-block write test","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-30T22:34:51.329212384Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-30T22:34:55.108364487Z","closed_at":"2026-03-30T22:34:55.108079077Z","close_reason":"Tests added and passing","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-4hzfa","title":"add mounted-path coverage for ext4 FS_IOC_GET_ENCRYPTION_POLICY v1 and ENODATA paths","description":"Survey finding: old fscrypt policy lookup is implemented in `ffs-core` and dispatched in `ffs-fuse`, with unit tests for success, short-buffer rejection, and `ENODATA`, but there is no mounted-path proof in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs`.

Scope:
- Add mounted-path coverage for `FS_IOC_GET_ENCRYPTION_POLICY` against an ext4 image that advertises `ENCRYPT` and carries a legacy v1 fscrypt context.
- Add a mounted-path absence case that proves stable `ENODATA` behavior for an unencrypted inode or missing context.
- Assert ioctl-trace behavior so transport-layer `ENOTTY`/`EOPNOTSUPP` skips remain distinguishable from FrankenFS regressions.
- Update `FEATURE_PARITY.md` wording once operator-visible coverage exists.

Acceptance:
- Success and missing-policy cases are both covered through the real mounted path.
- The test output makes v1-only behavior explicit and auditable.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T03:16:17.346541483Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T05:42:07.268125643Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","fscrypt","fuse","ioctl","testing"]}
+{"id":"bd-4hzfa","title":"add mounted-path coverage for ext4 FS_IOC_GET_ENCRYPTION_POLICY v1 and ENODATA paths","description":"Survey finding: old fscrypt policy lookup is implemented in `ffs-core` and dispatched in `ffs-fuse`, with unit tests for success, short-buffer rejection, and `ENODATA`, but there is no mounted-path proof in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs`.

Scope:
- Add mounted-path coverage for `FS_IOC_GET_ENCRYPTION_POLICY` against an ext4 image that advertises `ENCRYPT` and carries a legacy v1 fscrypt context.
- Add a mounted-path absence case that proves stable `ENODATA` behavior for an unencrypted inode or missing context.
- Assert ioctl-trace behavior so transport-layer `ENOTTY`/`EOPNOTSUPP` skips remain distinguishable from FrankenFS regressions.
- Update `FEATURE_PARITY.md` wording once operator-visible coverage exists.

Acceptance:
- Success and missing-policy cases are both covered through the real mounted path.
- The test output makes v1-only behavior explicit and auditable.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T03:16:17.346541483Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T06:04:05.180256553Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T06:04:05.177806244Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","fscrypt","fuse","ioctl","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-4il8g","title":"Freeze exact FfsError parse and source/unicode display contracts","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-19T06:35:57.473092077Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T06:43:26.396614410Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T06:43:26.394499319Z","close_reason":"Froze exact FfsError parse and source/unicode display contracts and validated with rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ffs-error","golden-artifacts","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-4ogh","title":"Implement ext4/btrfs parity features: decompression, inline data, bigalloc, ZERO_RANGE","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-22T18:28:47.537528441Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T18:29:02.634096851Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T18:29:02.633525312Z","close_reason":"Implemented 4 new parity features:
(1) btrfs transparent decompression: ZLIB (flate2) + ZSTD in read path for inline and regular extents. Added ram_bytes field to BtrfsExtentData enum. LZO deferred (custom framing).
(2) ext4 inline data read: reads from inode extent_bytes + xattr_ibody when EXT4_INLINE_DATA_FL is set. Removed INLINE_DATA from REJECTED_V1 incompat set.
(3) ext4 bigalloc infrastructure: added cluster_ratio to FsGeometry (cluster_size/block_size for BIGALLOC, else 1).
(4) ext4 ZERO_RANGE fallocate: writes zero blocks to existing allocated extents in range. Mode 0x10 now accepted.
All tests pass (471 ffs-core, 360 ffs-ondisk, 142 ffs-alloc, 117 ffs-btrfs, 114 ffs-extent).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","ext4","parity"]}
 {"id":"bd-4q7p","title":"Fix mark_written count=0 edge case: creates zero-length extent and corrupts tree","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-26T01:35:19.522825993Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-26T01:41:13.588091160Z","closed_at":"2026-02-26T01:41:13.588068958Z","close_reason":"Added early return for count=0 in mark_written and punch_hole. Added tests confirming noop behavior.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
